ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Din Tai Fung Fragrant Chilli Sauce; FOODON_03301710 (hot sauce food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Chilli sauce) is the closest parent; no branded Din Tai Fung-specific code exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229961009 (Chili sauce) is the closest available concept; no branded product-level code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Din Tai Fung Fragrant Chilli Sauce is approximately one tablespoon (15g), used as a dipping condiment or flavour enhancer alongside dumplings, noodles, or rice dishes.

nutrient_source.title: Chili sauce, ready-to-serve — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 02055) and branded chilli sauce reference values
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Din Tai Fung Fragrant Chilli Sauce is a proprietary branded product sold in a 170g jar. No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy chilli sauce reference (NDB 02055) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for comparable commercial chilli sauces (e.g., USDA FDC ID 2103689, Chili Garlic Sauce). Values represent a reasonable central estimate for an oil-containing fragrant chilli sauce. Energy, fat, and sodium are the primary nutritional concerns for this product category.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of chilli sauce and condiment analogues — University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Din Tai Fung Fragrant Chilli Sauce. GI is estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous chilli-based condiments and sauces in the University of Sydney GI Database. Chilli sauces with moderate sugar content and significant fat and fibre typically fall in the low GI range (GI 30–45). Glycemic load per 15g serving = (35 × 2.25g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 0.8, classified as negligible. Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~950 mg per 100g; ~143 mg per 15g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit use of this sauce to very small amounts (≤1 teaspoon per meal) as even a single tablespoon serving contributes approximately 143 mg sodium, and cumulative condiment use can significantly elevate daily sodium intake beyond the recommended 2000 mg/day limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and small serving carbohydrate load (~2.25g per 15g serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Din Tai Fung Fragrant Chilli Sauce has a low glycemic index and a negligible glycemic load per standard serving, making it generally acceptable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when used in typical condiment quantities; however, the sugar content (~8g/100g) warrants attention if large amounts are consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content with predominantly unsaturated fats, but high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The sauce contains predominantly unsaturated fats (monounsaturated and polyunsaturated) which are not inherently harmful to cardiovascular health, but the high sodium content (~950 mg/100g) poses a meaningful risk for individuals with cardiovascular disease who must restrict sodium intake; use should be limited and factored into total daily sodium budgeting.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Din Tai Fung Fragrant Chilli Sauce (豐味香辣酱) is a commercially produced, oil-based chilli condiment sold in 170g glass jars by Din Tai Fung, the internationally acclaimed Taiwanese restaurant chain renowned for its xiaolongbao (soup dumplings). The sauce is characterised by a fragrant, moderately spicy flavour profile derived from a blend of chilli peppers, aromatic spices, garlic, and vegetable oil, with a smooth to slightly textured consistency. It is designed as a dipping sauce and flavour enhancer to accompany dumplings, steamed buns, noodles, and rice dishes. The product is available for retail purchase at Din Tai Fung outlets in Singapore, including Marina Bay Link Mall, and is priced at approximately SGD 19.62 per jar. Nutritionally, the sauce is energy-moderate (~180 kcal/100g) due to its oil content, with a meaningful fat contribution (~12g/100g, predominantly unsaturated), moderate carbohydrates (~15g/100g including ~8g sugars), and notably high sodium (~950 mg/100g). The glycemic index is estimated to be low (~35), making it suitable for most individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when used in standard condiment portions. The primary clinical concern is sodium content, which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The sauce contains no animal-derived ingredients and is suitable for vegetarians and vegans. It may contain traces of soy or sesame depending on production batch.

confidence_reason: Din Tai Fung Fragrant Chilli Sauce is a proprietary branded product with no exact entry in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central under this specific product name. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy chilli sauce reference data and cross-referenced with comparable branded chilli sauce entries in USDA Branded Foods. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable chilli condiments. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 02055, chilli sauce) and comparable USDA Branded Food entries for commercial chilli garlic sauces. No exact nutritional panel for this specific Din Tai Fung product was available in any approved primary source at time of processing. If the manufacturer's official nutrition label becomes available, values should be updated to exact figures and confidence upgraded to high. 2. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database for this food category. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source database. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a plant-based condiment containing no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally zero in plant foods. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this product contains no vitamin D-fortified ingredients and is a plant-based condiment. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a plant-based oil and chilli sauce with no animal-derived cholesterol-containing ingredients. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as the product uses vegetable oil without hydrogenation per standard formulation for this product category. 4. Halal status is listed as false/unknown as Din Tai Fung Singapore's halal certification status for retail products requires verification from MUIS or the restaurant directly. 5. GI is an analogous estimate; glycemic load per 15g serving calculated as (35 × 2.25) / 100 = 0.79, rounded to 0.8.
